focus: Map adjustable Public surfaces by read reliability and runtime reach.

label: Root AGENTS is highest-reliability universal doctrine but already used by round 1.
Constitutional memory is fit for triggerable rules, not broad governor prose.
Prompt-time hook vocabulary spans Claude/Codex/OpenCode and matches governor re-injection needs.
Skill-advisor hook injects compact prompt-safe routing context before response and fails open.
OpenCode plugin bridge mutates output.system and has TTL, timeout, and disable controls.
Codex hook support is registration-sensitive and has timeout fallback semantics.
Agents are point-of-role surfaces for targeted ritual deltas.
Commands are workflow-entry surfaces and should get owned routes or workflow checks only when relevant.

direction: Scatter new doctrine across every agent and command first
reason: Lower reliability and higher drift than prompt-time hook delivery.
